content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(F\) be a field, let \(n \geq 2\), and let \(R\) be the ring of \(n \times n\) matrices over \(F\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the only 2-sided ideals of \(R\) are \(\{0\}\) and \(R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that \(R\) contains a nonzero proper left ideal \(I\).

body.text: Give an example of each of the following, with some explanation.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: A UFD which is not a PID.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: A ring \(R\) with unit and a ring \(S\) with unit such that \(S\) is a subring of \(R\) but \(1_R \neq 1_S\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: A commutative ring \(R\) with unit and an irreducible element \(\pi \in R\) which is not prime.

body.text: Prove that the ideal \((X,Y)\) in the ring \(\mathbb{Q}[X,Y]\) is not a principal ideal.

Let \(R\) be a ring and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. Define
\[
\operatorname{Tor}(M)=\{x\in M:rx=0\text{ for some }r\in R\setminus\{0\}\}.
\]

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(R\) is an integral domain then \(\operatorname{Tor}(M)\) is a submodule of \(M\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a ring \(R\) and an \(R\)-module \(M\) such that \(\operatorname{Tor}(M)\) is not a submodule of \(M\). Prove any claims that you make.

body.text: Find a representative for each conjugacy class in \(\operatorname{GL}_2(\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z})\) consisting of elements whose order divides \(8\). (Note that we have \(X^4+1=(X^2-X-1)(X^2+X-1)\) in \((\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z})[X]\).)
Let \(L/K\) be an algebraic field extension and let \(R\) be a subring of \(L\) that contains \(K\). Prove that \(R\) is a field.
